method: Web-research fan-out across serverless cold-start research, GPU model-load systems, and conference artifact-evaluation repos / Zenodo. Records here are the UNIQUE finds from this stream; Azure/Huawei records that overlap other phase files are cross-validated, not duplicated.
headline: Real MEASURED cold-start DURATION data exists publicly — but only for CPU serverless functions (Huawei richest; maxday/lambda-perf; SeBS). For GPU/LLM: model-load duration, autoscaling events, and migration events are MEASURED behind closed doors and published as plots, NOT released as datasets (ServerlessLLM, HydraServe, HeteroScale). Of the 12 target signals, only #1 cold-start-duration has a genuine measured public dataset (and it is FaaS, not GPU); #2 model-load and #4 migration are measured-but-unreleased; the rest are at best inferrable proxies.

artifacts.row_count_if_known: daily JSON 2022-present; each file = all runtime/memory/arch combos, ~10 cold-start samples each
artifacts.notes: Verified raw JSON: per-runtime entries i=[individual cold-start durations ms], d=runtime, m=memory MB, a=arch, ad=avg init duration, acd=avg cold-start duration, mu=mem used. MEASURED per-invocation cold-start. CPU FaaS only, NOT GPU model-load. Synthetic continuously-probed benchmark, not production.

artifacts.paper: SeBS: A Serverless Benchmark Suite for FaaS Computing (Middleware'21, Copik et al.)
file_types: Zenodo artifact.tar.gz (3.9 GB) with data/ result.csv
artifacts.notes: Measured cold+warm exec times AWS/Azure/GCP/OpenWhisk 2020. CPU FaaS, NOT GPU. result.csv = perf-cost summaries; raw per-invocation granularity unconfirmed.

artifacts.contains_autoscaling_metrics: partial (creation_time/scheduled_time/deletion_time per instance = derivable scale events)
artifacts.notes: Columns: instance_sn, role, app_name, cpu/gpu/rdma/memory/disk request+limit, max_instance_per_node, creation_time, scheduled_time, deletion_time. Instance lifecycle EVENTS for GPU INFERENCE serving (DLRM), NOT per-request, NOT time series. Scaling INFERRABLE from create/delete timestamps. NEW: an actual GPU-inference serving trace, but instance-level. No cold-start/queue/cache/migration/tail-latency.

artifacts.paper: ServerlessLLM: Low-Latency Serverless Inference for Large Language Models (OSDI'24, Fu et al.)
file_types: code + benchmark scripts; no measurement CSVs
artifacts.notes: THE most relevant system for GPU model-load + live migration, but released CODE ONLY. README shows summary speedup table (SafeTensors vs ServerlessLLM 5.93-9.95x on H100/NVMe) — figures/aggregates, NOT a downloadable dataset. The single best candidate that did NOT release measured model-load data.

artifacts.paper: HydraServe / Towards Swift Serverless LLM Cold Starts (NSDI'26; arXiv 2502.15524)
artifacts.notes: Measures cold-start breakdown (container, library, CUDA init, model fetch, model load) + TTFT/TPOT but reports ONLY figures/SLO%/speedup ratios. Code repo exists (reconciles agent-1 'no repo found'); no dataset/Zenodo.

artifacts.paper: IceBreaker: Warming Serverless Functions with Heterogeneity (ASPLOS'22)
artifacts.notes: Downloadable ZIP labeled 'Scripts and data of IceBreaker.' Likely simulation + Azure-trace-derived inputs rather than freshly measured cold-start latency. NOT verified to contain measured cold-start CSVs.

artifacts.paper: A Machine Learning Approach to Live Migration Modeling (SoCC'17)
signal_category: migration (the ONLY measured migration dataset found)
artifacts.notes: 40k+ MEASURED VM live-migration samples (timing/perf). But VM-class migration (2017), NOT inference/model migration, and NOT freely downloadable — email-gated. Closest thing to a migration-event dataset.

signal_category: autoscaling + queue_state + replica_contention (MEASURED in production, NOT released)
artifacts.notes: Production autoscaling papers use queue depth, KV-cache utilization, decode-TPS as scaling signals but release ZERO trace data (proprietary ByteDance/cloud). Figures only. Confirms autoscaling-event datasets do not exist publicly for LLM serving.

cross_validation.azure_functions_2019: CONFIRMED not a cold-start source — docs explicitly state execution time 'does not include the cold start time'; aggregated not per-invocation.
cross_validation.huawei_2025: CONFIRMED strongest measured cold-start dataset (FaaS): ~11.9M cold-start events, per-event totalCost_cold_start/podAllocationCost/deployCodeCost/deployDependencyCost/schedulingCost (microsecond granularity).
cross_validation.azure_llm_2023_2024: CONFIRMED workload-only (TIMESTAMP, ContextTokens, GeneratedTokens) — zero operational signals.
cross_validation.gpu_model_load_public_data: CONFIRMED none — Splitwise/DynamoLLM/ServerlessLLM/HydraServe measured GPU/model-load internally, released only figures + the input-token Azure traces.
